failure_modes.text: Learners can drown in complexity when they are asked to carry the whole system before mastering the next digestible chunk.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when complexity exceeds current capacity.
failure_modes.mitigation: Chunk complexity into digestible pieces and release each one only when the learner demonstrates mastery of the previous chunk.

failure_modes.text: A scaffold can harden into bureaucracy when the temporary support never gets removed and autonomy never transfers.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When the Scaffold Never Comes Down.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in scaffold-removal checkpoints: define when temporary support should be withdrawn and ownership transferred, preventing the scaffold from hardening into permanent dependency.

failure_modes.text: Framework-led teaching can drift into model-imposition when the instructor’s favorite structure outruns evidence about what the learner actually needs.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Law of the Instrument (Maslow's Hammer)
failure_modes.mitigation: Test framework fit against the learner's actual gap: verify that the structure being imposed matches what the learner needs, not what the instructor prefers.

premortem_questions.text: What is the next chunk this learner can reliably master, and what part of the full problem still exceeds current capacity?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when complexity exceeds current capacity.

premortem_questions.text: Which support should still be present now, and which one has already become drag instead of a bridge to independence?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when temporary structure can later be removed.

premortem_questions.text: Is this information chunked and simplified enough to be useful for the intended audience without becoming useless accuracy?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is this information chunked and simplified enough to be useful for decision-making by the intended audience?

heuristics.text: Break complex work into smaller units and hand over responsibility gradually rather than asking for mastery in one jump.
heuristics.source_quote: I Do, We Do, You Do

heuristics.text: Sequence explanations, modeling, probing, feedback, and mini-tasks so support evolves with the learner’s actual progress.
heuristics.source_quote: Provision of Knowledge; Demonstration and Modeling; Questioning and Probing; Feedback and Correction; Task Restructuring.

heuristics.text: Keep the scaffold temporary by removing supports as soon as independent performance becomes possible.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when temporary structure can later be removed.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on chunking, handoff, and anti-permanent-support failure modes rather than on general teaching commentary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor pedagogy-program sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on staged support mechanics.
open_questions: The source supports broader workflow design, but the intervention chunks stay on staged learning transfer.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on cognitive-load-theory, blooms-taxonomy, and feynman-technique.
antagonists: The source is strongest against availability-style framework misuse, curse-of-knowledge, and fast-thinking oversimplification.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against desirable-difficulties and confirmation-bias.
